file_name: GWG HOLDINGS, INC. - ORDERLY MARKETING AGREEMENT.txt

perturbation.original_text: 1.2 It is the goal of the Seller Trusts to have all of the Shares sold, on a pro-rata basis, in three or more tranches (each a "Tranche") commencing not earlier than six (6) months after the Effective Date with the resale of all of the shares of Stock completed as soon as practicable after the Effective Date.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 It is the goal of the Seller Trusts to have all of the Shares sold, on a pro-rata basis, in three or more tranches (each a "Tranche") commencing not earlier than twelve (12) months after the Effective Date with the resale of all of the shares of Stock completed no later than one (1) year after the Effective Date.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction. Originally, the agreement stated the shares should be sold commencing no earlier than six months after the effective date and completed as soon as practicable. This perturbation changes the commencement to twelve months and completion to no later than one year after the effective date. This contradicts the original intent of selling the shares as soon as possible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the timeframe for selling the shares, extending the commencement from 'six (6) months' to 'twelve (12) months' and setting a completion deadline of 'one (1) year'. This directly contradicts section 4.1, which states that the OMA expires on the earlier of 'the first anniversary of the Effective Date' or when all shares are sold. The change introduces a conflict as it pushes back the earliest possible completion date, potentially making the OMA's one-year expiration insufficient for the sale of all shares, or creating a situation where the shares are not sold when the OMA expires. This creates a significant in-text contradiction regarding the term and termination of the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the first anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may be terminated with or without cause at any time after the Effective Date and without liability or continuing obligation by any of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors in their sole discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Term; Termination; Withdrawal of Bank. (a) This OMA shall expire upon the earlier of (i) the third anniversary of the Effective Date and (ii) the date that all Shares of Stock of the Seller Trusts as set forth on Schedule A hereto have been sold (the "Term"). Notwithstanding the foregoing, this OMA may only be terminated with cause at any time after the Effective Date and with liability and continuing obligation by all of the Parties hereto (i) by mutual written agreement of all of the Parties; and (ii) in writing by the Trust Advisors if there has been a material breach by GWG.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by modifying the term and termination clauses. The original text states the OMA expires on the first anniversary of the effective date. The changed text extends this to the third anniversary. Furthermore, the original text allows termination with or without cause, while the modified text requires cause for termination. This directly contradicts the flexibility previously allowed and makes termination more restricted. The original text does not allow liability or continuing obligation, while the modified text include liability and continuing obligation. This intext contradiction makes it unclear under what conditions the OMA can be terminated and for how long it is valid, potentially leading to disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EXHIBIT A FORM OF JOINDER The undersigned is executing and delivering this Joinder Agreement pursuant to that certain Orderly Marketing Agreement, dated as of December [___], 2018 (as amended, restated, supplemented or otherwise modified in accordance with the terms thereof, the "OMA"), by and among GWG Holdings, Inc., the Trust Advisors to the Seller Trusts listed on Schedule A thereto, and the Priority Holders listed on Schedule B thereto, and any other person or entity that becomes a party to the OMA in accordance with the terms thereof. Capitalized terms used but not defined in this Joinder Agreement shall have the respective meanings ascribed to such terms in the OMA. By executing and delivering this Joinder Agreement to the OMA, the undersigned hereby agrees, effective commencing on the date hereof, to become a party to, and to be bound by and comply with the provisions of, the OMA applicable to it as a holder of Shares, in the same manner as if the undersigned were an original signatory to the OMA. The undersigned acknowledges and agrees that Section 4.1 through Section 4.11 of the OMA are incorporated herein by reference, mutatis mutandis.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term and termination clauses, creating a direct contradiction with the original text. The original text allows termination with or without cause, while the changed text requires cause for termination. Furthermore, the original text does not allow liability or continuing obligation, while the changed text include liability and continuing obligation. Exhibit A incorporates Section 4.1 through 4.11 of the OMA by reference, meaning that any changes to Section 4.1 directly affect the terms of the Joinder Agreement. The changed terms directly contradict the incorporated terms, creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, the Seller Trusts shall offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. GWG shall be entitled to appoint, in its discretion, an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.5 After the Parties have agreed on the strategy for the marketing, sale and distribution of a Tranche, GWG shall offer the Bank the right to serve as the lead left joint-book-running manager in connection with a best efforts distribution. The Seller Trust shall be entitled to appoint, in its discretion, an additional bank as joint book-running manager to participate in the distribution.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by switching the roles of GWG and the Seller Trusts in offering the lead book-running manager position and appointing an additional bank. Originally, the Seller Trusts offered the lead role, and GWG appointed the additional bank. The changed text reverses this, giving GWG the power to offer the lead role and the Seller Trusts the power to appoint the additional bank. This contradicts the original agreement and causes confusion over who has the authority to make these key decisions in the distribution process.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.6 Each such Distribution Agreement shall set out the customary terms and conditions for the sale and distribution of the respective Tranche, including customary representations, warranties, covenants and indemnification provisions. For the avoidance of doubt, each Distribution Agreement shall include provisions to the following effect: (i) GWG shall have no responsibility for the payment of fees or commissions payable to the Bank, which fees and commissions shall be the responsibility of certain affiliates of the Seller Trusts as set forth in the applicable Distribution Agreement; and (ii) each of GWG and the Seller Trusts shall agree to provide a customary indemnity in favor of the Bank and its affiliates.
perturbation.justification: The change in 1.5 directly contradicts the intent and allocation of responsibility detailed in 1.6. Section 1.6(i) explicitly states that GWG has no responsibility for fees payable to the Bank, and these fees are the responsibility of the Seller Trust affiliates. The original text in 1.5 supports this structure by having the Seller Trusts offer the bank the lead role. However, the changed text flips this, giving GWG the right to offer the lead role. This undermines the fee responsibility allocated to the Seller Trusts' affiliates in 1.6(i), as the offering party could implicitly influence fee negotiations. By changing who offers the lead role, the changed text potentially alters who is implicitly in control of the relationship and thus, contradicts the explicit allocation of fee responsibility outlined in 1.6(i). This creates a direct conflict, impacting financial obligations.
